reasoning: The unsafe finding (inline-bash.ts) is an example extension not executed during package build/install. AUR safety focuses on makepkg-time execution, not optional user-facing features. Qwen's finding is a false positive in this context.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen flagged an example extension allowing arbitrary command execution, while DeepSeek missed this file. The disagreement stems from whether example code constitutes a supply-chain risk.
coverage_issues: DeepSeek failed to review src/pi/examples/extensions/inline-bash.ts while Qwen did
false_positives: inline-bash.ts: Example extension not invoked during build/install, normal for development tooling
learnings: File selection should deprioritize example/ directories unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Improve context about AUR threat model in prompts - distinguish build-time vs user-facing risks
Implement example/test file filtering heuristics to reduce false positives
